items.title: Empire Premium bets on affordability to drive growth amid rising costs
items.summary: Co-founder Nicole Lim said the company's long operating track record has enabled it to navigate ongoing geopolitical disruptions and cost volatility, supported by a flexible menu strategy that helps cushion fluctuations in input costs. The company's pricing strategy, supported by its grab-and-go-based model and streamlined operations, allows it to keep costs relatively low while maintaining accessibility for mass-market consumers, she said after the company's prospectus launch on Wednesday. The IPO comprises 218 million new shares and 145 million existing shares, bringing the total offering to 363 million shares.

items.title: Contrarian investing success
items.summary: CONTRARIAN investing is an investment strategy that involves going against prevailing market sentiment. While most investors tend to follow trends—buying when prices are rising and selling when they are falling—contrarian investors deliberately do the opposite. At its core, contrarian investing is rooted in the belief that markets are often driven by emotion rather than rational analysis, leading to mispricing that disciplined investors can exploit.

items.title: Markets await Middle East stability for sustained gains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A clearer sign of normalisation in the Middle East is needed for the local equity market to post a sustainable positive momentum, said economists. They said the global equities market continues to depend on the trajectory of the conflict in Iran, which is still largely uncertain at this stage. Tradeview Capital fund manager Neoh Jia Man said the market is pricing in a relatively swift resolution to the Middle East conflict within the coming weeks.

items.title: Oil price outlook: Q2 dip before rising on Middle East risk, says Fitch
items.summary: The firm kept its forecast for Brent crude to set at an annual average of US$70/bbl for this year. "This aligns with our country risk team's base-case scenario of a four-week conflict and assumes a relatively rapid post-conflict recovery, with limited lasting damage to critical upstream production and export infrastructure and a two-to-four-week normalisation of flows through the Strait of Hormuz," it said in a note. However, the firm noted the rising risk of a longer-duration war, assigning a 35 per cent probability to their 'extend-to-end' scenario, which lasts up to eight weeks, and a 25 per cent probability to their 'extend-to-escalate' scenario, lasting multiple months.

items.title: Fatal LaGuardia incident underscores risks of runway incursions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A runway collision involving an Air Canada Express regional jet and a fire truck at New York's LaGuardia Airport on March 22 has drawn attention to runway safety procedures and air traffic control coordination. Early reports indicate both the Air Canada Express aircraft and the fire truck may have received clearance to use the runway. He said pilots relied on air traffic control to ensure the runway was clear once landing clearance was issued.

items.title: Weak enforcement of fireworks sales sparks safety fears
items.summary: news@nst.com.my KUALA LUMPUR: A safety activist has sounded the alarm over the mushrooming of fireworks stalls in public spaces, warning that weak enforcement and poor safety practices could lead to a tragedy. K. Bala Supramaniam said many such stalls appeared to be set up without proper consideration for safety, citing unsafe demonstrations in crowded areas, including pedestrian walkways and shoplots. He said traders were operating directly in front of business premises, including restaurants where patrons often smoke outside, next to the stalls selling fireworks.

items.title: Experts: Urn thefts inflict deep emotional, cultural harm on families
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The theft of over 50 urns is not just a property crime, but also a case of "weaponising grief", said experts, warning that existing laws fail to capture the full extent of the harm inflicted. Datuk Dr P. Sundramoorthy said such cases went far beyond conventional property offences, carrying deep psychological, cultural and emotional consequences. He said Section 380 addressed the act of theft primarily in material terms, overlooking the profound symbolic harm involved.

items.title: Cops probe possible link in urn theft cases at three memorial parks
items.summary: The suspects — two Malaysians and two foreigners — were picked up in connection with the theft of 22 urns from the Perpetual Memorial Park in Kulai, Johor. Similar thefts were also reported at the Xiao En Memorial Park in Nilai, Negri Sembilan, where 30 urns were stolen, and at the Nirvana Memorial Park. Kulai police chief Assistant Commissioner Tan Seng Lee said police had recorded statements from 23 people, including family members and memorial park staff.

items.title: UN names African slave trade 'gravest crime against humanity'
items.summary: UNITED NATIONS: The UN General Assembly on Wednesday designated the transatlantic African slave trade as "the gravest crime against humanity," in a move advocates hailed as a step towards healing and possible reparations. "The transatlantic slave trade was a crime against humanity that struck at the core of personhood, broke up families, and devastated communities," UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres said. The resolution went beyond simple acknowledgment, asking nations involved in the slave trade to engage in restorative justice.

items.title: Integrity at stake: Calls to revoke titles for convicted individuals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The revocation of federal and state awards, medals and honours should be seriously considered when a title holder is found guilty of a crime to protect the dignity of the award. He said they symbolised leadership, integrity and responsibility. "Honours carry moral weight and those who accept them must uphold exemplary conduct.

items.title: Experts push for clearer title revocation mechanisms
items.summary: This is to protect the integrity of the honours system and maintain public trust, said Malaysian Integrity and Governance Society president Datuk Seri Dr Akhbar Satar. Transparency International Malaysia president Raymon Ram said when title holders were implicated in serious criminal offences, particularly corruption or conduct that undermined integrity, the public might reasonably perceive that the honour itself had been diminished. He said when recipients of honorary titles face criminal allegations or convictions, the reputational impact extended beyond the person and could also weaken public confidence in the honours system.

items.title: Concrete capillaries: How hidden viaduct passages enable MRT cable thieves to access tracks in the Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Beneath the elevated tracks, a hidden network of hollow spaces within Klang Valley’s MRT viaducts is being exploited by cable thieves, inadvertently turning critical infrastructure into a pathway for intrusion. “In the past, cable thieves mainly targeted the ground-level tracks, but now they have also started attacking our elevated cables by entering through the viaducts and concrete piers. Hillside terrain and ground-level access near the Phileo Damansara station on the MRT Kajang Line provide natural cover and multiple informal entry points.

items.title: Brazil unveils first locally made supersonic fighter jet
items.summary: GAVIAO PEIXOTO: Brazil on Wednesday unveiled the first supersonic fighter jet to be produced locally, placing it among a small group of countries able to build such advanced aircraft. President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva christened the F-39E Gripen fighter jet from the Swedish group Saab with champagne during a high-profile ceremony in Gaviao Peixoto in the state of Sao Paulo. The Brazilian presidency said the building of the supersonic jet was "unprecedented in Latin America."

items.title: S. Korean 'drug lord' extradited from the Philippines
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korean police yesterday took into custody an alleged "drug lord" accused of running a narcotics ring in the country from a prison in the Philippines, said officials. South Korean authorities had said Park, believed to be 47, operated an alleged ring smuggling "large quantities" of illegal narcotics and conspiring with accomplices in the country to distribute them. Justice Ministry and police officials declined to comment on the scale or value of Park's alleged drug trade.

items.title: Musk's SpaceX looking to raise US$75b in June IPO
items.summary: NEW YORK: SpaceX, the company founded by tech billionaire Elon Musk, will soon file for an initial public offering in June that could raise US$75 billion to fund its space exploration plans, a report said on Tuesday. In recent months, analysts have expected Musk would seek to raise between US$30 billion and US$50 billion, given strong investor demand for technology firms. If the IPO achieves its target, it would surpass the previous largest offering on record: Saudi Aramco's US$25.6 billion IPO in 2019.

items.title: Water cuts dampen Raya cheer for Gua Musang folk
items.summary: Constant water cuts have dampened the Hari Raya celebrations for residents in Tanah Putih, here. Syahbuddin said water tankers provided only short-term relief. Tanah Putih resident Nur Hidayah Ramli, 35, said the family had to buy boxes of mineral water for daily use amid the water shortage.

items.title: 'El Nino may make 2026 hottest on record'
items.summary: A climatology and climate change expert is expecting global temperatures to hit a new high this year, driven by the El Nino phenomenon and global warming. El Nino refers to a condition of extreme environmental warming, where sea surface temperatures rise above average. This rise in sea temperatures triggers the onset of El Nino," he said.

items.title: Melaka police detain mother, four others over baby skeleton discovery
items.summary: MELAKA: A 19-year-old woman is among five individuals detained so far to assist in the investigation into the discovery of a baby's skeleton at a house compound in Kelemak, Alor Gajah, today. Melaka Police Chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the woman is believed to be the mother of the baby, whose birth was concealed. "All of them, including the woman believed to have given birth to the baby and her boyfriend, were detained shortly after the police received a report," he said.

items.title: Ex-junior hockey captain builds RM40mil business empire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Starting with a business capital of just RM30, he now possesses business assets worth approximately RM40 million. Such is the determination of the former national junior hockey captain, Muhammad Haziq Samsul, whose entrepreneurial efforts since his teenage years have paid off; he can now take pride in owning Bottol Garage Empire, a business offering motorcycle repair services as well as the sale of spare parts and accessories. Since then, Haziq - who has competed in prestigious tournaments including the 2013 Junior World Cup and the 2014 Commonwealth Games - has never looked back, now boasting nine Bottol Garage Empire branches across the country.

items.title: Man detained over Facebook post insulting Prophet Muhammad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police detained a 47-year-old local man suspected of insulting Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) over a Facebook post that recently went viral. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said a report was received at 1.30am last Sunday regarding the offensive post uploaded by an account using the name 'Sakti Sakti Sakti'. He said the suspect was arrested by a police team at 8.32pm yesterday in Butterworth, Penang.

items.title: Luxury Paris hotel near Elysee reopens after fire forces brief closure
items.summary: PARIS: A five-star hotel near the Elysee presidential palace in Paris reopened on Wednesday after a blaze earlier forced around 400 people to evacuate the building, the French capital's firefighters and the hotel said. Two members of staff were slightly injured as they tried to put out the fire which started at around 1020 GMT in the basement kitchen of the 114 Faubourg restaurant, owned by the Bristol hotel, often used by visiting world leaders close to the Elysee, the French president's official residence. The fire has been fully contained and extinguished, the Bristol hotel said in a statement later on Wednesday, adding that the hotel had been reopened.

items.title: ETS and KTM Komuter between Batu Caves and Pulau Sebang disrupted
items.summary: The fault affected the Batu Caves - Pulau Sebang -Batu Caves route, prompting service adjustments to ease passenger travel. ETS train EP9532 from JB Sentral to KL Sentral will now end at Seremban, with shuttle buses provided to KL Sentral and intermediate stations. Train 2047 from Batu Caves to Pulau Sebang will carry passengers by bus from Batang Benar to Seremban.

items.title: Seremban riders rush woman through Raya traffic to hospital for childbirth [WATCH]
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A man received a remarkable act of kindness from six motorcyclists while rushing his wife to Hospital Tuanku Jaafar (HTJ) in Seremban for childbirth on the third night of Hari Raya. Muhamad Syafiq and his wife, Auni Balqis Mohd Izan, 24, had just arrived at his in-laws' home in Chembong, Rembau, at around 8.30pm on Monday. He immediately helped open the way," Muhamad Syafiq said.

items.title: Trump names tech titans to science advisory council
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump on Wednesday named some of the biggest names in US technology to his Council of Advisors on Science and Technology, assembling a roster that includes Meta founder Mark Zuckerberg and Google co-founder Sergey Brin. The council, known as PCAST, will be co-chaired by Trump's AI and crypto adviser David Sacks and Michael Kratsios, a tech investor who previously served as the country's chief technology officer during the president's first term. The list of appointees includes Oracle chief Larry Ellison and Silicon Valley heavyweight Marc Andreessen, as well as Nvidia boss Jensen Huang and Lisa Su of chipmaker AMD.

items.title: France turns attention to 2027 presidential race
items.summary: WITH local elections done and dusted, France is pivoting towards electing a president to replace Emmanuel Macron next year, with the far right scenting its best chance yet to seize power. It is unclear how many presidential candidates the left will field next year, after the local elections that ended on Sunday. LFI leader Jean-Luc Melenchon, 74, is expected to launch another presidential bid, though he has not yet announced his candidacy.

items.title: 48 petrol stations identified as high-risk for subsidy leakage in Kelantan
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A total of 48 petrol stations operating within a 25-kilometre radius of the national border in Kelantan have been identified as high-risk for subsidy leakage activities. Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Kelantan Deputy Director Aswadi Jaafar said the ministry has increased the frequency of inspections at the identified stations to curb fuel misappropriation by irresponsible parties. "The implementation of Ops Tiris 2026 also focuses on inspections and enforcement of the ministry's regulations at public hotspots across the state.

items.title: Malaysia, Singapore share serious concern over Middle East situation - PM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and Singapore have expressed serious concern over the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on critical infrastructure following the escalating conflict in the Middle East, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. In a Facebook post, Anwar said the shared position was reached during a telephone conversation with Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong to discuss the latest developments in the conflict, which is increasingly affecting countries worldwide, including those in Southeast Asia. He said the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on critical infrastructure could disrupt global oil market stability and regional energy security, particularly in Southeast Asia.

items.title: Pipe burst disrupts water supply to 10,000 consumer accounts in Kelantan
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Air Kelantan Sdn Bhd (AKSB) has confirmed that Kampung Tok Deh and four other villages faced water supply disruption following a burst main pipeline on March 22. It said the incident involved a main pipe at Kampung Kelar, which channels treated water from the Kelar water treatment plant. "The disruption affected multiple areas supplied from the Lemal tank, including Pohon Tanjong, Kampung Merbau, Kampung Siram, Banggol Kong, Jeram Perdah, Kampung Chenarai, Kampung Lumpur, Lubok Kawah, Kedai Tanjong and Banggol Petaling, among others," it said.

items.title: Iran war's fuel crisis gives electric vehicles a long-term boost
items.summary: For example, the price of a litre of diesel in Australia has hit record highs around A$3, having risen about 36 per cent since the war started, while in Japan petrol has jumped by 18 per cent. Sales of EVs and PHEVs were around 12 million units in China last year, taking a more than 50 per cent share of new vehicle sales for the first time. Australia's sales of EVs and PHEVs hit a record high last year and accounted for about 12.7 per cent of total light vehicle purchases.

items.title: 14 Malaysian university subjects secure world's top 50 spots
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has recorded an 18 per cent improvement in the Quacquarelli Symonds World University Rankings 2026 for its course offerings. "Also performing excellently are the International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM), Universiti Malaya (UM), and Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM). Meanwhile, UM remains the highest-ranked university in Malaysia with 45 entries, including 18 within the world's top 100.

items.title: NST Leader: Indiscipline in enforcement agencies eroding public confidence
items.summary: On Tuesday, the Road Transport Department (RTD) suspended two of its personnel for allegedly being involved in reckless driving, one of which ended up in a fatal accident. This isn't the first time such incidents are being caused by RTD officers. One wonders what happened to the body cams mandated by the Transport Ministry for use by enforcement officers late last year.

items.title: JKR Sarawak says viral ‘vehicle damage claim’ checklist is fake
items.summary: SIBU, March 25 — The Sarawak Public Works Department (JKR) has denied issuing or circulating a document titled “Checklist of Documents for Vehicle Damage Compensation Claims” that has been shared on social media. In a statement released today by its corporate communications branch, JKR Sarawak stressed that the document is invalid and was not issued by the department. According to the statement, affected road users can contact the department’s official channels, including the Sarawak Public Works Department Facebook page, the TaliKhidmat portal at https://talikhidmat.sarawak.gov.my, the JKR Sarawak Care WhatsApp line at +60 11-7017 1777, or email corporatejkr@gmail.com for further complaints and enquiries.

items.title: Anwar welcomes Pakistan’s proposal to host US–Iran dialogue, says diplomacy must prevail (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has welcomed Pakistan’s timely and constructive offer to host a dialogue between the United States (US) and Iran amid a period of acute regional danger. Anwar said Malaysia supports the initiative and encouraged the US and Iran to respond in the spirit in which it was offered. The region deserves something more durable,” he said.

items.title: Malaysia’s universities improve in QS global subject score, but UM’s slide tells different story
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Malaysia’s universities recorded an overall 18 per cent net improvement in the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2026, but beneath that headline figure lies a story that is far more complicated. While specialist institutions like Universiti Teknologi Petronas and UCSI University surged forward, the country’s most comprehensive research university, Universiti Malaya, posted a net improvement rate of negative 25 per cent — the starkest divergence in the national results. UM entered more subjects than any other Malaysian university, fielding 40 narrow subject entries and five broad faculty areas, giving it the widest footprint in the rankings by some distance.

items.title: Fire Dept: Punggai Bypass bush fire 80pc contained as crews battle heat and strong winds
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, March 25 — Firefighting operations involving a bush fire covering an estimated 150 hectares along the Punggai Bypass Highway towards Sebana Cove have reached 80 per cent containment as of 1pm today. Punggai Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief Senior Fire Officer II Ezam Mistorerrodin said about 30 hectares remain active and are still being extinguished. He said the fire spread rapidly due to hot and dry weather, as well as strong winds, which have hampered firefighting efforts.

items.title: Malaysia’s UTP cracks global top 10 in historic QS university rankings breakthrough
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Universiti Teknologi Petronas has achieved what no Malaysian university has ever done before, ranking ninth in the world for Petroleum Engineering in the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2026. The landmark result, released by global higher education analysts QS Quacquarelli Symonds today, marks the first time any Malaysian institution has broken into the top 10 of any subject in the rankings’ sixteen-year history. QS senior vice president Ben Sowter credited the result to deliberate, long-term policy decisions “Malaysia’s rising performance in the QS subject rankings, including its highest-ever rank, achieved by UTP in Petroleum Engineering, reflects the long-term impact of reforms set out in the Malaysia Education Blueprint 2015-2025 and its continuation in the new Malaysia Higher Education Blueprint 2026-2035,” Sowter said in the announcement.

items.title: Sarawak police fast‑tracking investigation into Sri Aman crash that killed three
items.summary: KUCHING, March 25 — Police will expedite investigations into a fatal accident in Sri Aman that claimed the lives of three family members recently. Sarawak deputy police commissioner DCP Saifullizan Ishak said the investigation is being conducted from multiple angles. The accident on March 20 involved a Proton X70 and a Perodua Alza along Jalan Serian-Sri Aman.

items.title: Buying fuel in containers? You need a special permit, says Domestic Trade Ministry following viral drum‑filling cases
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 25 — Companies, organisations and individuals intending to purchase petrol or diesel at fuel stations for use outside vehicle tanks are required to obtain a Special Permit from the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) under the Supply Control Act 1961. “Permit holders may store the fuel in drums, plastic containers or other suitable and safe containers, subject to the approved quantity, fuel station location, duration and frequency of purchase,” he said in a statement today. “No parties other than Special Permit holders are allowed to purchase petrol or diesel in containers at fuel stations, except in emergency cases, where the permitted quantity is below 20 litres,” he said.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: Police followed SOPs in arresting teens over terror‑related activities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The Royal Malaysia Police’s (PDRM) action in arresting individuals, including underage teenagers believed to be attempting to revive a terrorist group ideology in the country, was carried out in accordance with standard operating procedures (SOP), said Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. Saifuddin Nasution said investigations found that the teenagers had been accessing websites related to violence. Meanwhile, in addressing potential long-term implications of the West Asian conflict, Saifuddin Nasution said the Cabinet has tasked the Home Ministry and the Defence Ministry with developing scenario planning to ensure national security remains intact.

items.title: Meru police station in Klang introduces bell‑entry system for after‑hours access following brawl
items.summary: KLANG, March 25 — As a security measure, the Meru police station is now using a bell system to control public entry after midnight, following a brawl at the station on March 2. North Klang district police chief ACP S. Vijaya Rao said this measure was taken to strengthen security aspects at the station, apart from ensuring the safety of police personnel and the public. “This measure is important because there are still many police stations across the country operating in shoplots like the Meru police station and requiring more effective security control.

items.title: Malaysia keeps subsidised RON95 at RM1.99, raises RON97 and diesel prices after global oil surge
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 25 — Malaysia will maintain the subsidised price of RON95 petrol at RM1.99 per litre under the Budi95 scheme, while prices of RON97 and diesel have been adjusted following a surge in global oil prices. Under the Automatic Pricing Mechanism, retail fuel prices for the week of March 26 to April 1 are set at RM5.15 per litre for RON97, RM3.87 for unsubsidised RON95, RM5.52 for diesel in Peninsular Malaysia, and RM2.15 for diesel in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan. The ministry said the government remains committed to protecting consumers despite volatile global markets, noting that Malaysia’s domestic fuel prices are influenced by international supply as much of it is imported.

items.title: No Malaysian peacekeepers harmed in south Lebanon explosions, says defence minister
items.summary: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the incidents occurred following tensions between the Israel Defence Forces (IDF) and Hezbollah in the area. “The Defence Ministry, through the Joint Forces Headquarters, is constantly monitoring the latest developments, particularly in MALBATT 850-13 operational areas. The safety of personnel remains a priority,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Mersing teen found drowned in Mersing river, body recovered 100m from last known location
items.summary: Mersing police chief Supt Abdul Razak Abdullah Sani said the body of Muhammad Adam Haziq Muhammad Hazriq was discovered at 12.41 pm. He said the Fire and Rescue Department’s Water Rescue Unit (PPDA) found the body about 100 metres from where the boy was reported to have gone missing, and at a depth of about six metres. Meanwhile, Endau Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) Operations Commander, Senior Fire Officer II Mohd Sahrel Anuar Mawarday, said they received an emergency call at 9.24 pm (on March 24) and dispatched six personnel to the scene.

items.title: Johor police nab sixth suspect in Southkey murder with help from Singapore
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 25 — Police have arrested another suspect to assist in the investigation into the murder of a man in Southkey here on March 14, bringing the total number of arrests so far to six individuals. Johor deputy police chief DCP Hoo Chuan Huat said the sixth suspect, a 30-year-old foreign man, was detained in Singapore this morning through close cooperation between the Royal Malaysia Police and the republic’s authorities. “A remand application for the suspect will be made tomorrow,” he told a press conference at the assembly held to commemorate the 219th Police Day at the Johor police contingent headquarters (IPK) here today.

items.title: Fahmi: PKR will fully cooperate in probe into RM10m ‘corporate mafia’ allegation involving its MP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — PKR is ready to fully cooperate with the authorities in connection with allegations of the party’s Member of Parliament’s (MP) involvement in misconduct involving a RM10 million transaction. PKR Information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the party’s leadership has been informed of the news reports on the allegations and leaves it fully to the authorities to carry out further investigations. “We have nothing to hide, and the party will cooperate with the enforcement authorities,” he told reporters when met after the 219th Police Day celebration at the Police Training Centre (PULAPOL) here today.

items.title: Sabah Police Gallery opens with rare exhibits from 2013 Lahad Datu intrusion
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 25 — The long-awaited Sabah Police Gallery features some never-seen-before photos and items from the Lahad Datu intrusion in 2013, one of the darkest spots in Sabah’s recent history. Launched today in one of the oldest buildings in the State police headquarters’ complex, the gallery includes the state’s history involving the police, including seized items from the intrusion in Tanduo, Lahad Datu, such as weapons, gear and personal belongings of the militants involved in the deadly incident. Sabah chief minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, who officiated the opening of the gallery today, described it as a “living archive” that preserves the history, hardships and commitment of the Royal Malaysia Police in Sabah and “not merely a place for exhibitions.” He said the gallery stands as a testament to the struggle, sacrifice and dedication of police personnel in maintaining national security.

items.title: MACC submits papers on DOE chiefs, targets graft tied to e‑waste and plastics
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 25 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is intensifying efforts to tackle corruption linked to the management of e-waste and plastic waste following recent progress in probes involving senior Department of Environment (DOE) officials. MACC Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said investigation papers involving the DOE director-general and deputy director-general have been completed and submitted to the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC) with a recommendation for prosecution. “Regarding the case involving the director-general and deputy director-general of the DOE, the investigation papers have been finalised and presented to the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC) with a recommendation for prosecution.

items.title: Fuel stations run dry in Victoria and NSW as Australian government cracks down on price manipulation
items.summary: SYDNEY, March 25 — Australia’s centre-left government on Wednesday introduced legislation in the parliament to double penalties for fuel price gouging as global supply disruptions due to the Iran war triggered fuel shortages in some rural regions, Reuters reported. Australia last year imported 84 per cent of its petroleum product demand, government statistics show, leaving it exposed to global shocks. According to Reuters, the legislation follows an investigation by Australia’s competition regulator last week into alleged anti-competitive conduct by major fuel suppliers, including Ampol, BP, Mobil Oil Australia and Viva Energy, which operates Shell and Liberty fuel stations.

items.title: PM warns against meddling in police action against major criminal syndicates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today issued a stern warning to influential parties against interfering in the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) in carrying out legal action, particularly in cases involving large-scale criminal syndicates. He said the tendency of certain parties to shield influential individuals or groups has, in the past, hampered the police force’s ability to enforce the law effectively. Present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah, and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.

items.title: Sarawak to develop Damai port for large international cruise ships, says deputy state minister
items.summary: KUCHING, March 25 — The Sarawak government will develop port facilities to accommodate international cruise liners carrying between 2,000 and 6,000 tourists per vessel at Damai, said Datuk Dr Abdul Rahman Junaidi. The Pantai Damai assemblyman said the state government has identified the constituency as one of the areas to be further upgraded for tourism. Among the main attractions under Pantai Damai are Telaga Air, Damai, and Santubong, including Pasir Pandak and Buntal.

items.title: Sabah prepared for dry spell, Hajiji urges caution amid hot weather
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 25 — Sabah is prepared to face extreme weather during the upcoming dry spell, but Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor has urged residents to exercise caution and take necessary precautions. He said the state’s disaster management committee is aware of the hot and dry conditions and is ready for any emergencies. Sabah is in the final phase of the Northeast Monsoon, usually characterised by reduced rainfall and hotter, drier conditions.

items.title: JKR: RM72.7b in 12MP projects delivered in Sarawak, including roads, bridges and schools
items.summary: KUCHING, March 25 — The total value of projects implemented under the 12th Malaysia Plan (12MP) in Sarawak has reached RM72.7 billion, covering 15,107 projects across the state. The Sarawak Public Works Department (JKR Sarawak) said the figure includes both state- and federal-funded initiatives, as well as those under Rural Transformation Projects (RTP) 2025. JKR Sarawak said the scale of implementation reflects the department’s capability in delivering infrastructure projects effectively, while reinforcing its commitment to strengthening development and improving the people’s quality of life.

items.title: Bau police issue two summonses to driver caught in viral dashcam clip for dangerous driving
items.summary: KUCHING, March 25 — A driver was issued two traffic summonses after being caught on a viral dashcam video driving dangerously along Jalan Bau-Kuching near Kampung Keranji, Bau on Sunday. Bau police chief Supt Mohd Haide A. Rahman said the incident, which occurred at about 5.34pm, involved a Perodua Kancil. Mohd Haide said the driver admitted to driving while drowsy after attending a religious event in Kampung Blimbing, Bau, and was travelling from Bau towards Kuching.

items.title: Home Minister says ‘corporate mafia’ probe ongoing, being carried out step by step
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Investigations into allegations of a “corporate mafia” are being conducted methodically, one step at a time, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today. Speaking at a press conference after the 219th Police Day celebrations at the Police Training Centre (Pulapol) here, Saifuddin said an investigation paper has been opened, with authorities first working to establish the direction of the probe before identifying the modus operandi and individuals involved. “Let us proceed step by step.

items.title: Religious affairs minister warns Muslims against normalising tattoos, stresses need for expert guidance
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 25 — Muslims have been advised to uphold religious boundaries and personal dignity, and to avoid practices that contravene Islamic law, such as tattooing, which should not be normalised in society. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan, in a statement today, reminded Muslims not to emulate actions that violate Islamic law. “Muslims must always safeguard religious limits and personal dignity, including refraining from acts that contradict Islamic teachings, such as tattooing, which should not be normalised.

items.title: Former Sabah minister Peter Anthony granted parole, returns home after serving part of cheating sentence
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 25 — Former Sabah state minister Datuk Peter Anthony, who is about a year into serving a three-year sentence for a cheating case at Kajang Prison, has reportedly been released on parole and returned home in the interior of Sabah. Deputy president of Parti Kesejahteraan Demokratik Masyarakat (KDM), Datuk Wetrom Bahanda, confirmed Peter’s release but declined to provide further details. Sabah police commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikon said parole matters fall under the Prison Department and declined to elaborate.

items.title: MetMalaysia: Inter‑monsoon phase to bring severe weather nationwide, with thunderstorms and possible hail
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The Monsoon Transition Phase is expected to begin this Sunday (March 29) and continue until May, leading to thunderstorms and heavy rains accompanied by strong winds in most areas in a short spell. Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said the areas expected to be affected are the west and interior of the Peninsular Malaysia, south and central Sarawak and the western part of Sabah. He said the phase would continue until May, marking the end of the 2025/2026 Northeast Monsoon which began on Nov 13 last year.

items.title: Drought disaster declaration lifted in Tawau, water supply in Sebatik still monitored
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 25 — Authorities have lifted the drought disaster declaration in Tawau following improved conditions, although water supply issues in Pulau Sebatik remain under close watch. In a statement, Tawau District Disaster Management Committee (JPBD) chairman Joseph Pang said the decision was made after recent assessments showed the situation no longer meets the criteria for a drought disaster. However, he noted that water supply shortages in Pulau Sebatik are still being closely monitored by authorities.

items.title: PM joins top leadership at 219th Police Day celebration at Pulapol, launches children’s crime‑awareness book
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today attended the 219th Police Day celebration at the Police Training Centre (PULAPOL) in Kuala Lumpur. He arrived at about 2 pm and was received by Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail, his deputy Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay and Bukit Aman police directors. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.

items.title: Anwar warns Malaysia cannot take national security for granted amid global tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today warned that escalating global tensions, including the ongoing conflict involving Iran, should serve as a reminder that Malaysia cannot afford to take its national security for granted. Speaking at the 219th Police Day celebrations at the Police Training Centre (Pulapol) here, Anwar said the situation was complex and required careful understanding, particularly by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), given the potential security implications arising from global instability. We must not view national security issues lightly in times of such turmoil,” he said.
